articles.expand: Prior week 4 week average 33K Current week's 4 week average The ADP NER Pulse is a newer high-frequency labor market indicator tied to the ADP National Employment Report (NER). articles.title: Dollar Slips as President Trump Cancels a Planned Attack on Iran
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) fell from a 1.25-month high on Monday and finished down by -0.25%. The dollar gave up overnight gains on Monday and turned lower following reports that the US proposed a temporary waiver of sanctions on Iran's oil. Also, comments on Monday afternoon from President Trump...

articles.title: The USD is higher to kickstart the trading day. Stocks pointing lower. Yields lower too.
articles.expand: The USD Is higher vs the major currencies to kickstart the new trading day. The biggest gains are vs the AUD (+0.64%) and the NZD (+0.49%). The greenback is up 0.30% vs the EUR, 0.17% vs the JPY and 0.22% vs the GBP. In the video above, I talk about the technicals in play for those 3 major currency pairs to kickstart the North American session. Rec...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Inflation spike seen as manageable – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole expects a sharp rise in Canada’s April Consumer Price Index (CPI), but sees limited pressure on the Bank of Canada (BoC) to hike.

articles.title: Canada CPI expected to show rising inflation in April, pressuring BoC rate outlook
articles.expand: The publication of Canada’s April Consumer Price Index (CPI) figures on Tuesday will be the focus of attention.

articles.title: Swiss Franc dips as rising US yields overshadow stronger Swiss growth
articles.expand: USD/CHF trades higher around 0.7870 on Tuesday at the time of writing, gaining 0.35% on the day as the US Dollar (USD) benefits from renewed support linked to expectations of a more hawkish monetary policy stance in the United States (US).

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: CPI strength seen above BoC track – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities expects Canada’s April CPI to firm to 3.1% year-on-year, driven by higher energy prices and base effects from the removal of carbon taxes.

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Rally pause as data focus shifts – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC’s FX Strategist Christopher Wong notes that the Dollar Index (DXY) has eased alongside lower US Treasury yields, with no tier-1 US data due today. Focus turns to upcoming FOMC minutes and US flash PMIs to gauge inflation persistence and activity momentum.

articles.title: British Pound underperforms against a broadly weaker JPY amid UK political crisis
articles.expand: The GBP/JPY cross attracts fresh sellers in the vicinity of mid-213.00s on Tuesday and erodes a part of the previous day's strong recovery gains from a one-and-a-half-week low.

articles.title: RBNZ: Gradual hiking path from September – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Prashant Newnaha now expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to start raising the Official Cash Rate (OCR) from the September 2026 meeting, having previously forecast the first move in February 2027.

articles.title: BoE: Softer labour market data shape rate path – Nomura
articles.expand: Nomura’s Josie Anderson, George Buckley, Andrzej Szczepaniak and David Seif highlight a softer UK labour market, with falling payrolls, rising unemployment and weaker vacancies. They stress that labour data lag the Iran war shock, but see current softness as the starting point.

articles.title: Gold seems vulnerable near daily low as Fed hike bets and geopolitical risks lift USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its offered tone through the early European session on Tuesday and remains well within striking distance of the lowest level since March 30, set the previous day.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Strong GDP fails to lift versus US Dollar – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank analysts note Japan’s economy grew faster than expected in Q1 2026, supporting the case for further Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hikes, yet the Japanese Yen (JPY) weakened slightly against the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Euro: Retest of 1.1600 against US Dollar seen likely – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole argues that while the European Central Bank (ECB) must stay hawkish to contain long‑end yields, EUR/USD remains vulnerable.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Weak UK Jobs Data Pushes BoE Toward Patience, but CPI Holds Key to Breakout
articles.expand: Sterling softened after the latest UK labor market data reinforced expectations that the Bank of England is likely to adopt a more patient near-term stance on interest rates. The sharp decline in payroll employment, rising unemployment rate, and cooling wage growth excluding bonuses all pointed to a labor market that is gradually losing momentum, r...

articles.title: Why the Pound is starting to trade like an emerging-market currency
articles.expand: The Bank of England (BoE) is being priced to hike around 70 basis points before year-end. The 10-year gilt is near 5.2%, its highest since the 2008 financial crisis. The 30-year sits at levels last seen in 1998. None of it is helping the Pound.

articles.title: US Dollar Index Price Forecast: Fresh upside likely if it breaks above 99.40
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) recovers on Tuesday after a sharp corrective move the previous day amid firm expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) will not cut interest rates this year.

articles.title: Aussie Dollar Slips as RBA Signals Pause After Rate Hikes
articles.expand: RBA minutes from the latest meeting showed that after three rate hikes this year, members could now be ready for some pause after three hikes this year. They may want to wait for more data and also see how the situation in the Middle East develops, so they likely do not want to rush too much with additional hikes for now and may need more time. Thi...

articles.title: Fed: Rate cuts pushed out as risks rise – BNY
articles.expand: BNY strategists John Velis and David Tam have abandoned their call for two Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, citing persistent disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and a labor market that has not weakened as expected.

articles.title: Norwegian Krone: Energy shock supports strength – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Antje Praefcke at Commerzbank highlights that the Norwegian Krone (NOK) has outperformed Euro (EUR) and US Dollar (USD), helped by Norway’s status as a net Oil and gas exporter during the Iran war.

articles.title: FX Watch: GBP/CHF and EUR/GBP Breakouts for an Upbeat U.K. CPI Report
articles.expand: The U.K. CPI report lands just after the jobs release, and hotter than expected data could be enough to revive a hawkish BOE bias. Here’s what I’m watching on GBP/CHF and EUR/GBP in this scenario.

articles.title: RBA Minutes: Tactical Wait-and-See, Not End of Tightening Bias Yet
articles.expand: Minutes from the RBA’s May meeting suggested policymakers are leaning toward a pause in June after acknowledging that interest rates are now likely in restrictive territory. The Board raised the cash rate by 25bps to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote, but members also judged that “financial conditions would probably be somewhat restrictive after this decision,”...

articles.title: Australian Westpac Consumer Confidence Ticks Higher, but Energy and RBA Rate Fears Still Dominate
articles.expand: Australia’s consumer sentiment improved modestly in April as lower fuel prices helped ease part of the energy shock that hit households earlier this year, though confidence remained deeply pessimistic overall. articles.title: Event Guide: U.K. CPI Report (April 2026)
articles.expand: The U.K. inflation reports lands a day after the jobs release, likely keeping the focus on U.K. fundamentals while the economy also navigates political uncertainty. Can the CPI figure push sterling in a strong direction?

articles.title: Central Bank to launch GIFT city branch next month: MD
articles.expand: Central Bank of India is set to launch its IFSC Banking Unit at GIFT City next month with an aim to expand its international banking operations. The bank will offer specialised services and access to international financial markets for corporate clients.

articles.title: IMF Tells BoE to Stay Flexible on Rates as Middle East Shock Tests UK Economy
articles.expand: The IMF upgraded UK 2026 growth to 1% while urging the Bank of England to hold rates at 3.75% yet stay ready to cut if economic conditions warrant. Fresh analysis highlights how Middle East energy shocks have upended earlier easing expectations and forced policymakers to weigh inflation risks against growth concerns.
